verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1) is predominantly a cytoplasmic/nuclear chaperone under normal conditions. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the plasma membrane surface under stress conditions (heat shock, oxidative stress, hypoxia) and on tumor cells, where it can be detected on the outer leaflet. This surface form is accessible to antibodies and has been targeted by immunotherapy approaches. Surface HSP70 appears to be membrane-anchored (associated with lipid rafts/ceramide-rich domains), making it wash-resistant in the surface state. Thus, surface localization is cell-state-induced rather than constitutive.
